summary: A solid mid‑90s show with a scorching Midnight Hour and strong newer material, captured in a decent download that listeners find pleasant.
review: The performance is highlighted by an incendiary first‑set “Midnight Hour,” a rare first‑set debut of “So Many Roads,” and powerful renditions of “Broken Arrow,” “Eternity,” and “Liberty.” Reviewers note the extra percussion from Sikiru Adepoju and Mickey Hart while Billy Kreutzmann sits out partway, adding an unusual texture. No audio flaws are reported, making the recording a worthwhile listen despite its unknown source type.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 4–5) and enthusiastic comments about both the music and the listenability of the download indicate a strong show with good recording quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners did not mention any audio problems; the download is described as pleasant and worthwhile, suggesting clear enough sound despite no explicit source information.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A hot first set opens with a killer “Midnight Hour,” followed by an eclectic second set that includes rare and newer material, ending with high energy that was uncommon for the era.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Fans recall a powerful emotional connection to Jerry, describing the show as magical and unforgettable.
band_member_comments.Phil: Multiple reviewers highlight “Phil bombs” and his bass driving the vibe, especially during the “Midnight Hour.”
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart is noted for playing alongside guest percussionist Sikiru Adepoju while Billy Kreutzmann sits out partway; an assistant reportedly sat in on drums.
